experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for the active O piece at the top center. With an empty field and next pieces I,T,S,Z, the best immediate objective is efficient stacking and preserving flexibility. Since O does not benefit from rotation and is already near the center, moving it one column left or right is optional; the strongest simple play is to place it quickly on the bottom to start a flat stack. hard_drop achieves this fastest with no downside on an empty board.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is almost empty except for a 2x2 O block resting in the center bottom at columns 4-5. The active I piece is horizontal near the top spanning columns 3-6. Goal is to place pieces efficiently and keep the stack flat while preserving flexibility for upcoming T,S,Z,J. A horizontal I dropped now would land across the top of the O block and create a broad flat surface with only shallow side wells, which is safer than rotating vertical and making a tall spike this early. Left/right only shifts the eventual flat placement slightly; immediate hard drop advances fastest toward stable placement.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is mostly empty, with a flat-ish stack at the bottom: an I piece spanning row 17 and an O piece on the right at rows 18-19. The active T is at the top in its spawn orientation. Goal is to place pieces cleanly to keep the surface even and avoid creating holes; with an empty board, fast downward progress is best. Rotating or shifting now is premature without a clearer need, and a hard drop from spawn in this orientation would likely create an awkward high placement over the bottom structures. A soft drop advances the piece while preserving flexibility for later horizontal movement/rotation based on where it will best fit.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is mostly empty with a flat-ish stack near the bottom: an I piece on row 18 from columns 3-6 and an O piece at bottom center-right, creating a small 2-wide raised area. The active T is high and safe, so there is no immediate threat. Goal is to place pieces to keep the surface even and prepare for upcoming S, Z, J, L pieces. A good sub-goal is to move the T toward the center-right to potentially fill around the O block and avoid stacking over the left I unnecessarily. Candidate actions: left moves toward the I and may create awkward overhangs; rotate actions are premature since there is ample time and no immediate fit needed; dropping now would commit too early. Right starts shifting the T toward the O side, preserving flexibility for later rotations and placement.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is mostly empty with a flat stack near the bottom: an I lying across row 18 and an O block at the bottom center-right. The active T piece is high up, so there is no immediate threat. Goal is to place pieces efficiently for a clean stack and preserve flexibility for upcoming S,Z,J,L. Since the piece is still high, the best sub-goal now is to start moving it toward a useful landing zone rather than dropping blindly. A T often fits well near the center-left around the existing I/O structure, and shifting horizontally now costs nothing. Candidate actions: hard_drop commits too early with little setup; soft_drop reduces maneuver time unnecessarily; rotations are not yet needed because there are no constraints overhead. Moving left begins positioning the T toward a more useful central placement and keeps all later options open.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is mostly empty, with a flat I piece on row 18 spanning columns 4-7 and an O block centered below it on the right. The active T is high and unobstructed, so there is no immediate threat. The goal now is to place the T to build a clean, flat stack and avoid creating holes before the upcoming S/Z/J/L pieces. Candidate moves: shifting left or right repositions for future placement; rotating is unnecessary yet because no tight fit is needed; hard dropping immediately would likely leave the T floating awkwardly on the existing center structure. A soft_drop advances toward a controlled placement while preserving flexibility next turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The stack is very low: an I-piece lies horizontally on row 18 at columns 4-7 and an O sits centered at the bottom on rows 19-20, cols 5-6, leaving broad flat space. The active T is high above the stack in spawn orientation. Goal is to place it cleanly without creating holes and ideally support upcoming S/Z/J/L. Dropping immediately in current center position would likely land around the O/I area and can fit reasonably, but shifting first can improve flexibility. Moving left now starts guiding the T toward the left side where it can rest on the flat I surface and preserve central/right options for the awkward upcoming S and Z pieces. Other actions: right would crowd the O side, rotate is not yet necessary on open board, no_op wastes time, and hard/soft drop commits too early from a high position.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is very low and safe: only a flat I on row 18 and an O on the bottom right create a small platform, while the active T is high with no immediate threat. Goal is to place the T to improve the surface for upcoming S,Z,J,L pieces and avoid creating holes. Since the current spawn is centered and there is ample time, the best immediate sub-goal is to start moving the T toward the left side where it can later fit cleanly above the I/O structure without blocking the center. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit too early from a high position; soft_drop reduces flexibility; rotations are not yet necessary because current flat T orientation is already versatile; right moves toward the O stack and is less attractive. So the best action now is to shift left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is mostly empty with a flat stack near the bottom: an I piece lying across row 18 and an O on the right at the floor. The active T is high and centered. Immediate threat is low, so the goal is efficient placement to build a clean surface for upcoming S, Z, J, L pieces. Dropping the current T in its spawn orientation now would likely place it flat above the existing stack, creating a manageable contour without wasting time. Lateral movement or rotation may improve placement later, but with only one action now, the strongest progress toward locking the piece into useful space is to accelerate it downward.

experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active L piece is high near the center in vertical orientation. The stack below is low and mostly centered, with no immediate danger of topping out. The best sub-goal now is efficient placement toward the left side to keep the center/right flexible for upcoming S and Z pieces. A single move left starts shifting this L toward a cleaner landing zone; rotating or dropping now would commit too early, while no_op wastes a turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high above the stack with no immediate danger. The surface has a left-side notch around the T/SS area and a flatter center-right over the I and O. My goal is to place the L to improve surface smoothness without creating overhangs; since only one action is allowed now, I should start shifting toward a useful landing zone. Rotating now is unnecessary because the current vertical-right-foot orientation can be useful, and moving left begins aligning it with the left-side contour better than staying or moving right. Soft/hard drop this early would commit too soon.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active L piece is high on the left in vertical orientation with its foot to the right. The stack below is low and uneven, with more structure in the center-right and open space on the far left. Immediate threats are minimal since the board is still relatively low, so the main opportunity is to place this L efficiently to preserve a flat surface and avoid creating overhangs. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit immediately in the current column, likely creating a rough left-side bump; soft_drop also advances downward without improving alignment; rotations could make the piece horizontal, but without first shifting it left it may not best use the wall/open space. Moving left brings the piece toward the left wall, where an L can be tucked more cleanly and keeps options open for later rotation or drop. So the best sub-goal now is to position the L at the far left before committing.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

Goal is to place the L to improve the left surface without creating holes; the best immediate sub-goal is to move it further left and likely drop it vertically into the far-left column area. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would commit to a suboptimal placement at current x; rotations are unnecessary because the current vertical-L shape is useful for the left wall; right moves away from the open target. experiences.intentions: Active L piece is high and unobstructed; stack below has a left wall and a central/right mound with a clean 2-wide well at the far left upper area. Immediate goal is to place the L efficiently without creating overhangs, while setting up for upcoming S,Z,T,I. Moving left starts guiding the L toward the left side where it can likely slot alongside the existing left column and keep the center flexible. Rotating now is premature since multiple orientations may be useful later; dropping now would commit to a poor high placement. So the best single-step action is left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S at the top, currently horizontal over columns 4-5 with open space below. Surface below has a left-side notch around rows 13-16 near columns 3-5 and generally uneven stack, but there is no immediate threat at the top. Goal is to place the S efficiently to smooth the center-left surface and preserve flexibility for upcoming Z,T,I,J. Immediate horizontal movement is more valuable than dropping now, since hard/soft drop in current column would create a high central bump. Rotating vertical now could target a notch, but first shifting left helps line it up with the central-left cavity. Candidate actions: left starts moving toward the useful placement zone; right moves away into emptier right side; rotate now without horizontal alignment is premature; dropping now commits badly. Best action is left.

experiences.intentions: The active S piece is near the top center in its horizontal orientation. The stack below has a shallow cavity around columns 3-5, with the highest occupied cells much lower, so there is no immediate threat. My goal now is to position the S to fit cleanly into the central contours and avoid creating overhangs. Candidate moves: soft_drop or hard_drop would commit too early while still far above the stack; rotating to vertical is possible but not clearly better yet; moving left shifts the S toward the central-left notch formed above the T/L area, which is a promising setup and keeps options open. Therefore, moving left is the best preparatory action.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is S near the upper middle-left, currently horizontal at rows 4-5. The stack below has a notable left-side cavity around columns 2-3 above the lower L/T structure; dropping the S in its current orientation won’t fit cleanly there, but rotating clockwise to vertical creates the shape needed to slide/fall into that notch on subsequent moves. Immediate goal is to orient the piece for a cleaner placement and avoid an awkward flat drop on top of the stack. Candidate actions: left/right only shift without improving fit yet; soft/hard drop would likely commit to a poor surface placement; rotate_left would be equivalent but rotate_right is explicitly shown valid to the useful vertical S orientation.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is an S in vertical orientation near the upper middle, with open space below and some surface irregularities around columns 3-5. The stack is low overall, so the priority is improving board shape for upcoming Z,T,I,J pieces rather than emergency survival. The existing terrain has a dip around the center-left above the J/Z/S layers; shifting left and later dropping the vertical S can help knit into that area more cleanly than staying centered. Candidate actions: soft/hard drop commits too early before horizontal alignment is optimized; rotation would make it horizontal but not yet clearly best at current x; right moves away from the useful center-left pocket. Moving left is the best setup action now.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is Z near the top, currently horizontal with columns around 4-6. Surface below has a notch on the left-center; immediate danger is low because stack is only mid-height. Goal is to place the Z cleanly to avoid creating holes and prepare for upcoming T/I/J/O. Candidate moves: hard_drop would place the current horizontal Z onto the existing uneven center-right surface, likely making a decent fit; lateral moves first are possible but without exact landing simulation they risk worse overhangs. Rotation to vertical near current column seems less useful for the broad central contours. Since there is no top-out threat and dropping efficiently secures the piece, best sub-goal is to lock it in a reasonable spot now. Therefore choose hard_drop.

experiences.intentions: Active T is spawning near the top in flat-up orientation over a board whose upper half is completely empty, so there is no immediate collision threat. The stack below has some irregularities but no urgent top-out danger. Goal now is to position the T efficiently for future line clears without committing too early. Since there is ample vertical space, the best sub-goal is lateral placement before dropping. Candidate actions: hard_drop would lock the T immediately onto a likely suboptimal central surface; soft_drop also commits downward before horizontal alignment; rotations are unnecessary because current flat T is flexible and there is no tight slot to fit yet; no_op wastes a turn. Moving left begins steering toward the more open left-center terrain and preserves options for later rotation/drop.

experiences.intentions: The active T is high near the top with plenty of time; the stack is uneven but the main accessible feature is a left-side notch around rows 13-16 that can likely accept a T if moved left over subsequent turns. Immediate goal is to start positioning the T toward a useful landing area while avoiding committing early with a drop. Candidate actions: left moves toward the left-side cavity and keeps all options open; right moves away from the best visible fit; rotations may help later but are unnecessary now; dropping is premature from this height. Best action is left.

experiences.intentions: The active T is high near the left-center with plenty of air below. The stack is uneven, with a notable left-side trench around columns 0-2 and a central-right structure that is not immediately fillable by this T from its current position. Goal is to place the T efficiently without creating overhangs while moving toward the accessible left gap. Rotating now is unnecessary because the current flat-top T can still be shifted, and dropping now would commit too early. Moving left starts aligning the piece with the left-side cavity and preserves future rotation/drop options.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a T near the upper-left with plenty of air below. The stack is higher on the left/middle and there is a vertical trench around columns 2-3 above the lower pieces. My goal is to place the T to smooth the surface and avoid creating overhangs. A hard drop now in the current orientation would likely land awkwardly on the uneven left stack, while moving left can line the T up over the left-center cavity for a stronger future placement. Rotations are possible later, but first shifting left improves access to the gap.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is T near the upper left, currently spanning columns 0-2 with center at row 6/7. Surface below has a useful notch around the left-center: row 17 has L..IIII..., row16 ..TTT....., row18 L...OO...., leaving an accessible vertical channel near columns 1-2 above the stack. Immediate goal is to place the T cleanly without creating overhangs and ideally fill the notch on the left side. Candidate actions: left shifts piece toward the wall to line up for that channel; right moves away from the best fit; rotations could prepare a tuck later but first horizontal alignment is more urgent while piece is high; dropping now would commit too early. Best sub-goal is move left now to approach the left-side slot.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is T near the upper-left. Surface below is uneven, with a useful notch around columns 2-4 above the lower stack; preserving flexibility matters since no immediate line clear is available. A soft/hard drop in current orientation would likely leave the T stranded high on the left and create overhangs. Rotating to the vertical T (clockwise) improves the chance to slide into a cleaner slot on subsequent moves while still high enough to maneuver. Immediate sub-goal is to orient the T for placement without sealing holes.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is a T near the upper-middle left, currently vertical with blocks around rows 8-10. The surface below is uneven, with a central valley and some overhang risk; there is no immediate line clear available from dropping now. My goal is to place the T to improve the stack and avoid creating holes while setting up future I/J/O/L pieces. Among actions, moving left/right would only shift before deciding orientation, soft/hard drop would commit to the current awkward vertical placement, and no_op wastes time. Rotating right to the horizontal T orientation appears to better match the nearby contour and gives more flexibility for a later drop into the central area, so the best immediate action is to rotate_right.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is T in rotation 3, sitting around rows 10-12 with support below. The surface has a left-side notch near columns 0-3 and a broader cavity structure in the center-left, while the right side is relatively flatter but already occupied by Z/J. Goal is to place the T to improve surface smoothness and avoid creating overhangs before upcoming I,J,O,L pieces. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would lock the current awkward vertical T where it is, which is poor. Rotating right would move to rotation 0, creating a wider horizontal top but not yet improving placement. Moving left shifts toward the left notch, but one step may not yet align. Soft drop commits downward too early. The best immediate sub-goal is to reorient the T into a flatter shape that can then be slid into a cleaner slot; rotation 0 provides a horizontal bar with center stem, generally stronger for fitting onto the uneven center-left surface than the current vertical shape.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I horizontally near the top at columns 3-6 with a clear shaft below around column 5. Existing stack is low-to-mid and there are no immediate threats at the top. Goal is to place the I efficiently, ideally vertically into a gap to improve future line clears and avoid wasting the long piece flat on an empty top area. Candidate actions: left/right only shift the horizontal I and don’t exploit the vertical opportunity yet; soft/hard drop now would place it flat uselessly; no_op wastes a turn. Rotating right turns the I vertical in an open space and is the best setup toward dropping it into the central channel next.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a vertical I at column 5 near the top. Surface below is irregular, with a deep open vertical lane on the far left and some central clutter; there is no immediate threat at the spawn area. Goal is to place the I efficiently, ideally preserving flexibility and avoiding creating overhang problems. Candidate actions: rotate would make it horizontal too high at spawn and not clearly better; soft/hard drop commits immediately without first aligning to a useful column; no_op wastes a turn. Moving left starts steering the I toward more useful placements, especially the left-side well or central gaps, while keeping options open next turn.

experiences.intentions: Active I is vertical in column 4 near the top. Surface below has a deep 1-wide well at column 3 with uneven bumps; keeping the I vertical and moving left can target that well for a strong placement on a later drop. Rotating horizontal now would sit high across columns 2-5 and is less useful. Immediate goal is to align the I with the column-3 gap before dropping. Among actions, left advances toward that setup; right moves away, rotations worsen fit, and dropping now would commit too early.

experiences.intentions: Active I is vertical in column 3 near the top. Surface below has a deep open vertical lane at column 3 down to row 16, while lower rows are irregular. Goal is to place the I cleanly to improve the stack and potentially set up future line clears. Candidate actions: left/right would shift away from the best-fit lane; rotation to horizontal near the top risks poorer placement over bumps; soft_drop advances but still allows adjustment later. Hard_drop immediately locks the vertical I into the open column, which is the strongest placement now.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is near the top center with an open field below; current stack has uneven surface with a left-center well and some clutter on the right. Goal is to place the J efficiently without committing too early, ideally moving toward the left/center where it can support future O/L/I pieces and avoid building over the rough right side. Candidate actions: hard_drop is premature from spawn height; soft_drop reduces flexibility; rotations are not yet necessary because the board is open and horizontal J is fine for sliding; right would steer toward the messier side. Moving left begins positioning toward the more useful central-left area while preserving options.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active J piece is high near the spawn area with plenty of time before danger. The stack below has a broad flat top around rows 12-16 with some unevenness on the left-center, while the far right is relatively low and the upcoming O,L,I,Z suggest keeping the center open is useful. Immediate goal is to move the J toward a cleaner placement rather than hard-dropping from the current central-left spawn. From spawn, shifting right starts routing the J toward the open right side and avoids committing to a poor central placement; rotating now is less urgent because horizontal J already moves well, and dropping now would waste flexibility.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high and centered above a rough stack with a notable left-side trench and mid-right clutter. Immediate goal is to place the J without creating worse overhangs and to move toward flatter surface for upcoming O/L/I. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would place current horizontal J high over center, likely awkward. Rotating now could make a vertical J useful, but first shifting left better targets the left-center gap and keeps options open for later rotation/drop. Soft drop commits downward too early; right seems less useful due to existing bumps. Best sub-goal is reposition left before locking into orientation.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is J at the top-left cluster around rows 6-7, and only its current orientation is valid at the present x,y, so rotation is not useful now. The immediate opportunity is to move it toward a cleaner landing area; below its current column there are stacked blocks creating an uneven surface, while one column to the right appears more open and better aligned for future placement without locking immediately. Goal is to improve placement options and avoid committing to a poor drop. Among candidates, left moves toward existing clutter, hard/soft drop would likely place suboptimally now, and no_op wastes a turn. Moving right best advances toward a safer, potentially flatter landing zone.

experiences.intentions: Current board shows the active piece is O at the very top center (rows 1-2, cols 5-6). O does not benefit from rotation. The stack is uneven but the center column area beneath the spawn is relatively open down to around the J/ZZ structure; immediate goal is to place the O efficiently without creating overhangs and ideally keep the surface flat for upcoming L, I, Z, S pieces. Candidate actions: rotate_left/right do nothing useful for O; no_op wastes a turn; left or right could reposition, but there is no urgent need and shifting may create a worse fit near uneven edges. soft_drop advances but hard_drop secures the piece in the natural central landing spot quickly and safely. Best sub-goal now is to lock the O into the central gap to preserve board simplicity.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the top around columns 4-6 with open space below. Stack is uneven but not in immediate danger; the left side has a tall wall and the center-right has a usable cavity structure. Goal is to place the L efficiently without creating overhangs and keep the surface workable for upcoming I,Z,S,J. Candidate moves: hard_drop now would commit the current horizontal L at x≈4 into the central area without alignment; left/right could shift toward cleaner fits but need more setup; rotation can make the L vertical, which often fits narrow channels better. Rotating right to orientation 1 creates a vertical L shape that can be guided into the center-left gap on subsequent moves while preserving flexibility. Best immediate sub-goal is improve piece orientation before descent.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.
